model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Wisconsin is the safer pick because its full-season predictive profile is clearly stronger, sitting around the top 20 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik while High Point is closer to the low 90s. The Badgers also bring a major edge on offense and were installed as roughly a 9.5-point favorite, which matches the underlying quality gap. High Point has a credible upset path with its 30-win season, 14-game winning streak, and enough scoring to make this a high-variance game, but Wisconsin’s stronger schedule, better two-way efficiency, and no obvious major availability red flag make it the more reliable advance choice.

reasoningStep.title: Compared team quality, health context, and upset path
reasoningStep.summary: Wisconsin rates far better in predictive metrics and enters as a solid market favorite, while recent reporting did not reveal a major injury issue strong enough to swing the matchup. High Point is dangerous because of its record, winning streak, and capable offense, but its weaker defense and much lower overall efficiency profile make the upset path real yet narrower.
evidence: Wisconsin ratings: KenPom No. 22 (AdjEM 23.4, AdjO 125.4 No. 11, AdjD 102); Torvik No. 21 (AdjO rank 8, AdjD rank 48).
High Point ratings: KenPom No. 92 (AdjEM 8.4, AdjO 117.1 No. 66, AdjD 108.7); Torvik No. 93 (AdjO rank 73, AdjD rank 148).
Market/preview search results listed Wisconsin as about a 9.5-point favorite with a high total around 164.5.
Recent web results referenced Wisconsin injury concerns earlier in March for Nolan Winter and Max Klesmit, but follow-up reporting indicated return optimism rather than a clear tournament absence.
High Point enters 30-4 and on a 14-game winning streak, which supports a legitimate underdog path if it turns the game into an efficient scoring contest.

result.text: Wisconsin vs. High Point: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Wisconsin vs. High Point: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for Wisconsin vs. High Point. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: Wisconsin vs. High Point Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Wisconsin 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Greg Gard has built one of the more fascinating two-sided profiles in the field. On offense, Wisconsin plays at a Final Four level. Nick Boyd has been one of the most consistent players in a loaded conference all year, earning All-Big Ten second-team honors. John Blackwell as the second scorer gives the Badgers two engines who can get hot simultaneously, as they showed in a monster road win at Purdue, where the two combined for 48 points, and in a win over Illinois in the second round of the conference tourney, where they combined for 69 points. Mix in Austin Rapp and Braeden Carrington's ability to microwave from deep, along with the third-lowest team turnover percentage in the nation, and it adds up to one of the best offenses in D-I hoops. This is a team that went 7-3 ATS as an underdog this year. The Badgers live and die by the three, with over 42% of their points coming from deep, fourth-most in the nation, which can be good and bad. When Wisconsin shot under 33.3% from deep this year, they went 3-8. They went 21-2 in all other games, including wins over Purdue, Michigan, Illinois, and Michigan State. The problem is the defense, which ranks 50th in adjusted efficiency. Wisconsin was been beaten handily in games where the offense didn’t show up early in the season. Against non-con opponents, the Badgers went 8-3 and dropped to 49th overall before figuring out their offense. Another concern entering the tourney is Nolan Winter’s ankle injury, which has left him day-to-day. Per Hoop Explorer, Winter has one of the biggest impacts on team efficiency in all of

result.text: Wisconsin vs High Point prediction, pick, odds March Madness first round Skip to main content Home Sports Packers Brewers Bucks Badgers Golden Eagles Panthers Preps Plus Outdoors Sports Awards Motor Sports Wisconsin basketball vs High Point prediction: Our pick for 2026 NCAA bracket first round John Steppe Milwaukee Journal Sentinel Show Caption Hide Caption An aerial view of the Kohl Center on the campus of UW-Madison An aerial view of the Kohl Center in Madison, Wis., home of the Wisconsin Badgers. MADISON − Wisconsin knows all too well about the dangers of the classic No. 5/No. 12 upset in the Men's NCAA Tournament . Back in 2024, the fifth-seeded Badgers fell in the first round to 12th-seeded James Madison. It was an abrupt end to a 22-win season with major contributions from AJ Storr, Steven Crowl, Tyler Wahl and others. Greg Gard's 2025-26 group will try to avoid repeating history as the current fifth-seeded Badgers will face 12th-seeded High Point in the first round on March 19 in Portland. The Panthers have won their past 14 games en route to earning the automatic bid from the Big South. "I always say, 'Erase the numbers,'" Wisconsin coach Greg Gard said of the seeding. "They're the conference champion in the Big South. They've had a terrific year. Most of their losses were early. ... When we were a one, when we were the two, I wasn't any less nervous." Here are three things to watch in Wisconsin's first-round matchup and our prediction: Buy Wisconsin NCAA tournament tickets vs. High Point How does Wisconsin defend High Point’s veteran duo? Veteran contributions from Terry Anderson and Rob Martin have been key parts of High Point's 30-win regular season. Anderson and Martin will also be a key test for a Wisconsin defense that had an impressive stretch against Michigan in the Big Ten Tournament semifinals and some woes against inferior foes such as Oregon. Anderson, a 6-foot-6 forward, began his career at the junior-college level before spending two years at Lamar. Now as a graduate transfer at High Point, he leads the Panthers with 16 points per game while shooting 56.4% and averaging six rebounds per game. Martin, a 5-foot-10 guard who transferred from Southeast Missouri, is not far behind Anderson with 15.3 points per game while also shooting 42.2% from the field. He has shown the ability to be a menace on the defensive end, too, as evident when he recorded eight steals in the Panthers' Feb. 4 win over Charleston Southern. How does Wisconsin manage pace of play against another up-tempo team? Wisconsin has pushed the tempo to a level that it has not operated at in past seasons in the Bo Ryan and Greg Gard eras. The Badgers' adjusted tempo, as measured by KenPom.com, currently ranks 104th in the NCAA and in the top third of the Big Ten. But for as fast as Wisconsin has operated, High Point has operated even faster. The Panthers' adjusted tempo is 49th in the country, and their average offensive possession length is 24th in the country. UW

result.text: Wisconsin vs. High Point: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Wisconsin vs. High Point: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for Wisconsin vs. High Point. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: Wisconsin vs. High Point Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Wisconsin 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Greg Gard has built one of the more fascinating two-sided profiles in the field. On offense, Wisconsin plays at a Final Four level. Nick Boyd has been one of the most consistent players in a loaded conference all year, earning All-Big Ten second-team honors. John Blackwell as the second scorer gives the Badgers two engines who can get hot simultaneously, as they showed in a monster road win at Purdue, where the two combined for 48 points, and in a win over Illinois in the second round of the conference tourney, where they combined for 69 points. Mix in Austin Rapp and Braeden Carrington's ability to microwave from deep, along with the third-lowest team turnover percentage in the nation, and it adds up to one of the best offenses in D-I hoops. This is a team that went 7-3 ATS as an underdog this year. The Badgers live and die by the three, with over 42% of their points coming from deep, fourth-most in the nation, which can be good and bad. When Wisconsin shot under 33.3% from deep this year, they went 3-8. They went 21-2 in all other games, including wins over Purdue, Michigan, Illinois, and Michigan State. The problem is the defense, which ranks 50th in adjusted efficiency. Wisconsin was been beaten handily in games where the offense didn’t show up early in the season. Against non-con opponents, the Badgers went 8-3 and dropped to 49th overall before figuring out their offense. Another concern entering the tourney is Nolan Winter’s ankle injury, which has left him day-to-day. Per Hoop Explorer, Winter has one of the biggest impacts on team efficiency in all of

Wisconsin vs High Point picks, predictions for March Madness NCAA game Skip to main content Home Schedule NFL News Draft Fantasy Odds Betting Newsletter USA TODAY Sports Wisconsin vs High Point picks, predictions, odds for March Madness Jeremy Cluff Arizona Republic Show Caption Hide Caption March Madness Final Four predictions and national champion picks From chalk favorites to hot upsets, the panel shares Final Four picks and bold calls on who cuts down the nets. No. 5 seed Wisconsin and No. 12 seed High Point play Thursday, March 19, in a March Madness NCAA Tournament first-round game . The West Region game is scheduled for 10:50 a.m. MST and can be seen on TBS ( stream with Sling TV ). Who will win the March Madness game and advance to the second round in the NCAA Tournament bracket ? Check out these March Madness picks and March Madness predictions for the men's college basketball NCAA Tournament matchup . Wisconsin is a 9.5-point favorite over High Point in the game in March Madness odds provided by BetMGM Sportsbook . The Badgers are -500 on the moneyline. The Panthers are +375. The over/under for the game is set at 164.5 points. It shouldn't overlook High Point with a potential clash against Arkansas on deck." March Madness picks: Predictions for NCAA Tournament first-round games Dimers : Wisconsin 85, High Point 75 It writes: "After extensive simulations, our model gives High Point a win probability of 19%, while Wisconsin has a win probability of 81%." Join the madness: USA TODAY Bracket Challenge | Survivor Pool | Printable bracket ESPN : Wisconsin has a 76.3% chance to beat High Point The site gives the Panthers a 23.7% shot at defeating the Badgers in the NCAA Tournament March Madness first-round game. Bookies.com : Bet Wisconsin to cover vs High Point Bill Speros writes: "The 12-seed upset narrative has legs. Just not here. High Point (30-4) is legitimately hot and owns the nation's top winning streak at 14 games. This number means the Badgers expect Nolan Winter back at center. And a fully healthy Wisconsin (20-14 ATS) lineup means one-and-done for the pesky Panthers." March Madness odds: Ohio State-TCU | Nebraska-Troy | Louisville-South Florida | Wisconsin

Wisconsin vs High Point prediction, pick, odds March Madness first round Skip to main content Home Sports Packers Brewers Bucks Badgers Golden Eagles Panthers Preps Plus Outdoors Sports Awards Motor Sports Wisconsin basketball vs High Point prediction: Our pick for 2026 NCAA bracket first round John Steppe Milwaukee Journal Sentinel Show Caption Hide Caption An aerial view of the Kohl Center on the campus of UW-Madison An aerial view of the Kohl Center in Madison, Wis., home of the Wisconsin Badgers. MADISON − Wisconsin knows all too well about the dangers of the classic No. 5/No. 12 upset in the Men's NCAA Tournament . Back in 2024, the fifth-seeded Badgers fell in the first round to 12th-seeded James Madison. It was an abrupt end to a 22-win season with major contributions from AJ Storr, Steven Crowl, Tyler Wahl and others. Greg Gard's 2025-26 group will try to avoid repeating history as the current fifth-seeded Badgers will face 12th-seeded High Point in the first round on March 19 in Portland. The Panthers have won their past 14 games en route to earning the automatic bid from the Big South. "I always say, 'Erase the numbers,'" Wisconsin coach Greg Gard said of the seeding. "They're the conference champion in the Big South. They've had a terrific year. Most of their losses were early. ... When we were a one, when we were the two, I wasn't any less nervous." Here are three things to watch in Wisconsin's first-round matchup and our prediction: Buy Wisconsin NCAA tournament tickets vs. High Point How does Wisconsin defend High Point’s veteran duo? Veteran contributions from Terry Anderson and Rob Martin have been key parts of High Point's 30-win regular season. Anderson and Martin will also be a key test for a Wisconsin defense that had an impressive stretch against Michigan in the Big Ten Tournament semifinals and some woes against inferior foes such as Oregon. Anderson, a 6-foot-6 forward, began his career at the junior-college level before spending two years at Lamar. Now as a graduate transfer at High Point, he leads the Panthers with 16 points per game while shooting 56.4% and averaging six rebounds per game. Martin, a 5-foot-10 guard who transferred from Southeast Missouri, is not far behind Anderson with 15.3 points per game while also shooting 42.2% from the field. He has shown the ability to be a menace on the defensive end, too, as evident when he recorded eight steals in the Panthers' Feb. 4 win over Charleston Southern. How does Wisconsin manage pace of play against another up-tempo team? Wisconsin has pushed the tempo to a level that it has not operated at in past seasons in the Bo Ryan and Greg Gard eras. The Badgers' adjusted tempo, as measured by KenPom.com, currently ranks 104th in the NCAA and in the top third of the Big Ten. But for as fast as Wisconsin has operated, High Point has operated even faster. The Panthers' adjusted tempo is 49th in the country, and their average offensive possession length is 24th in the country. UW
